content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To : Jim Stevens, Director                      Date : June 28, 1982
     Political Reform Audit Bureau
     Franchise Tax Board
     P. O. Box 651


From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
       Robert M. Stern, General Counsel

Subject: Period of Audit


     Thank you for your June 17, 1982 memo concerning
auditing of statewide candidates who are also legislators.
You have asked over what period of time should the audit
cover.

     Section 90002 (b) requires that the audit cover all
campaign statements filed since the last election for that
office. Thus, if the candidate for statewide office has
filed a campaign statement for that statewide office,
e.g., Committee to Elect X Attorney General, then the
audit covers the period since the beginning date of the
first statement. If the candidate has legislative
campaign committees, the audit should cover the period
since the candidate's last election. Therefore, an
audit of an assemblyman running for statewide office
would include the period from January 1, 1981 to June 30,
1982. The audit would not go back to the candidate's
last audit.


RMS:cs

State of California                                                    Franchise Tax Board

Memorandum

To    : Bob Stern,General Counsel                          Date  : June 17, 1982
        Fair Political Practices Commission
                                                           File No : JS:ks

From  : Jim Stevens

Subject: Period of Audit - 1982 Election


        Section 90002(b) of the Government Code provides that the audit and inves-
tigation shall cover all campaign statements and reports filed in connection
with an election and any previous campaign statement filed since the last
election for that office. There is an apparent conflict here with Section
90001(c) which provides for random audit selection.

        We have noted that in this 1982 State election there are a number of can-
didates who are currently senators or assemblypersons running for statewide
offices and also a number of candidates who are currently assemblypersons
running for state senate offices. Once a senator or assemblyperson has been
subjected to random selection and his/her district was not selected for audit,
do we have the authority to audit all the statements filed since the last
election for statewide office or senate office, including those campaign
statements that were subjected to random selection but were not selected?

        For example, a statewide candidate is currently an assemblyperson. The
statewide candidate has been an assemblyperson since 1976 and the last
audit was for period ending 12/31/78. There was no audit of the 1980
assembly campaign because his/her district was not randomly selected.
Assuming the statewide candidate is a 1982 primary loser, would the audit
period be 1/1/79 through 6/30/82 (because of Section 90002(b)) or 1/1/81
through 6/30/82?


        [signature]
        Director, Political
        Political Reform Audit Bureau
